definition: the context is all the other words that were said or written at the same time as the word you are trying to understand taken in/out of context
example: Read the quote in the correct context so that the meaning is clear.

definition: a consequence is the result of a specific action or choice
example: There are always consequences for our actions.

definition: something that is extreme has a large amount or is very serious and unusual
example: The heat can be extreme in the desert.

definition: something that is flexible can change easily to different situations
example: Our working hours are flexible as long as we work 40 hours per week.

definition: a houseboat is a large boat that is turned into a home on water
example: Harry lives on the river in a houseboat.

definition: a charity is an organization that helps people who don’t have money, food, or homes
example: The Red Cross is a charity that has helped many people over 100 years.

definition: if a place is messy, it is dirty and/or untidy
example: Their house was very messy and hadn’t been cleaned in days.

definition: a pile is many things put on top of each other
example: He found an old photo between a pile of books.

definition: stuff is a general word used to refer to a group of things or ideas without saying exactly what they are
example: What’s the sticky stuff on the carpet?

definition: if you throw away things, you no longer want them
example: Don’t throw away that food; I’ll eat it later.

definition: if you tidy up a place, you make it look clean and neat by putting things in the right place
example: Please help me to tidy up these papers in my office.

definition: a method is a way of doing something, usually in a way that has certain steps to it
example: Alicia created a new method to bake the cake.

definition: trash is something that is not valuable or needed anymore
example: Please remember to empty the trash before you go to work.

definition: a series is a group of TV or radio shows that have the same characters or story
example: The new series on TV is so funny.

definition: something that is best-selling has more people buying it than other things like it during a specific time
example: Jake’s book is the best-selling one of the year!

definition: something that is life-changing makes a big change to the way you think, do, or feel about things around you
example: Jelena’s decision to become a teacher was life- changing for her.

definition: if you recommend that someone does something, you tell them you think it’s a good idea to do it
example: Can you recommend a good dictionary?

definition: when you do something for emotional reasons, you do it because you have strong feelings about it
example: Shevehenko made an emotional return to his first soccer club.

definition: an item is a single thing on a list or part of a set of things
example: You may pay for each item separately.

definition: when you recycle things like paper, plastic, and glass, you clean them in a special process so that they can be used again
example: The Japanese recycle more than half of their waste paper.

definition: windsurfing is a sport where you stand on a board that has a sail and the wind blows into it to move you along
example: Khairi wants to try windsurfing when he goes to Australia.

definition: a takeout is a meal that you buy from a restaurant and take it to eat somewhere else
example: Let’s order takeout for dinner tonight.

definition: something that is spectacular is very surprising, exciting, or good
example: This morning’s sunrise was spectacular.

definition: karaoke is a type of entertainment with just music so you can sing the words of the songs
example: Let’s do some karaoke tonight after dinner.

definition: air conditioning is a system in cars and buildings that makes the air cooler
example: You have to have air conditioning in Daegu, South Korea, because it gets so hot in the summer.

definition: if you allow someone to do something, you let them do it
example: This hotel does not allow people to bring their pets.

definition: an opportunity is a chance you have to do something or go somewhere
example: Jibri has an exciting opportunity to move to New Zealand.

definition: something that is weird is unexpected, unusual, or strange
example: Khairi thought it was weird when he couldn’t find his keys on the table.

definition: something that is disgusting is extremely unpleasant
example: The smell of rotten fish is disgusting.

definition: something that is enormous is very big in size or number
example: The Pacific Ocean contains an enormous amount of water.

definition: something that is filthy is extremely dirty
example: Kara’s hands were filthy after working in the garden.

definition: something that is freezing is very, very cold
example: It’s freezing tonight. The temperature is below 0 degrees celsius.

definition: something that is worse is more unpleasant or difficult than something else that is also bad get worse
example: Koban’s headache is worse today than it was yesterday.

definition: a survey is a set of questions you ask people as part of a scientific study
example: Ivanna is busy doing a survey as part of her research.

definition: a mission is a purpose or specific task that someone does
example: The navy is on a search and rescue mission.

definition: a place that is remote is usually small and far away from cities
example: Anya lives in a remote village with 1,000 people.

definition: a tent is a shelter you can sleep in, made of metal poles and cloth and ropes
example: We slept in a tent on the beach when we went windsurfing this weekend.

definition: a hammock is a bed made of canvas or net that is hung between two trees or poles outdoors
example: Wendy likes to relax in her hammock and watch the ocean.

definition: a teammate is someone who is in the same group as you in sports
example: Fakir has 10 teammates in his soccer team.

definition: a host is a person who entertains guests either at the host’s home or somewhere else
example: Ivanka was a great host to Alexis and Cade when they visited her in Slovakia.

definition: a hostel is a cheap type of accommodation for travelers
example: Elif and Tova stayed in hostels while doing a bike trip through Thailand.

definition: a bench is a long seat for two or more people and is usually made out of metal or wood
example: Rai likes to read on a bench in the park near her apartment.

definition: someone who is shocked is very surprised or upset
example: Natania was shocked to see how much the price of gas had increased this month.

definition: if you persuade someone to do something, you talk to them a lot about it until they agree to do it
example: Leonard couldn’t persuade Sheldon to take the bus to work that morning.

definition: a capsule hotel is a very small room, with no bathroom, that is only big enough for a bed
example: The capsule hotel Iderse stayed in in Tokyo was tiny but comfortable.

definition: bed and breakfast hotels or guest houses are a room to sleep in with a meal in the morning for a set price
example: We stayed in a great bed and breakfast for the weekend in Swansea.

definition: flexibility is when you can change or be changed easily as needed by a situation
example: The flexibility of distance learning is perfect for people working and studying at the same time.

definition: when you catch up with someone, you see them again after not seeing them for a long time and talk about what you have been doing
example: Let’s get together this Saturday and catch up over coffee.

definition: if you say you make yourself at home, you mean you feel relaxed and comfortable in a place like it’s your own house
example: When Annicka arrived at the house, Shaina said, “Make yourself at home.”

definition: if you tell someone help yourself, you mean you are giving them permission to do or use something
example: Please help yourself to sugar and milk for your tea.

definition: someone who is annoyed is angry or upset annoyed at, annoyed with, annoyed by
example: Yasira was annoyed with Ivor when he didn’t respond to her emails at work.

definition: someone who is talkative talks a lot
example: Shoshana is very talkative and talks to everyone.

definition: if you say you have time for something or someone, you mean you can use an amount of time for something have time for, have time to
example: Do you have time to go to the supermarket for me?

definition: if you look forward to something, you feel happy or excited for it to happen
example: Eliska is looking forward to moving to Israel next month.

definition: if you prioritize something, you choose to do it first before anything else
example: Being able to prioritize tasks well is a good skill to have.

definition: something that is magnificent is very good or beautiful
example: Deidre has an absolutely magnificent garden.

definition: a terrace is a flat area outside a house or building where you can sit and relax
example: The office has a beautiful terrace where we can eat our lunch.

definition: an arrival is the time you appear at a place
example: Our flight’s arrival time is set for 18:00 tomorrow.

definition: if you pick up something, you collect it from someone
example: Taj will pick up the keys from you next Tuesday. provide prəˈvaɪd/ verb if you provide something, you give people something they need at a specific time We will provide lunch during the workshop.

definition: check-in is the process you do when you arrive at a hotel, rental, etc. where you give your details and get the key for your room
example: Check-in can be done between 14:00 and 18:00.

definition: if you arrange something, you make a plan to do it
example: Let’s arrange a time to go and watch a movie together.

definition: if something is nearby, it is close and not far away
example: Is there a bus stop nearby?

definition: a rental is a house or apartment that you can pay money to stay at on vacation or live in
example: Catalina is looking for a rental for six months while she works in Prague.
